column1: # sanitize-line-rules.tsv — line-level drop rules used by sanitize_ai_text
# step 3 (see hooks/lib/common.sh). Lines matching `pattern` are removed
# from the AI text BEFORE the dialog reaches Haiku, so transient noise
# (stack frames, shell output, error labels) doesn't leak into titles.
# - id              short name; used in test failure messages
# - pattern         POSIX ERE regex; if a line matches, the WHOLE line drops
# - matching_example   one line that the pattern MUST drop
# - non_matching_example  one line of plain prose that MUST be kept
# Adding a new noise shape: append one row. test-sanitize.sh iterates this
# file and asserts both directions per row, so a faulty pattern is caught
# without touching code. Stateful logic (fence toggle, blank collapse,
# substance gate, length cap) deliberately stays in code (per design note
# Lines starting with `#` and blank lines are ignored.
